title: strychnos torresiana
intro: Strychnos torresiana, also known as the Torres' strychnine tree or Torres' poison tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae, native to the Philippines and Indonesia. The plant contains strychnine, a toxic compound that has medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often dried and made into powder or decoctions to treat these conditions, and it is also used as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. However, due to its toxic nature, the plant should be handled with caution and consumed only under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
